message:
fix(packs/kubernetes): tighten kustomize "safe build" regex + correct kubectl test assertion

Two small correctness fixes to the kubernetes packs that surfaced after
the new per-pattern coverage landed (acad49b / 0d2dec7).

src/packs/kubernetes/kustomize.rs
  Negative-lookahead in the "kustomize-build" and "kubectl-kustomize"
  safe patterns was `(?!\s*\|)` — only refusing to match when the pipe
  was immediately preceded by whitespace. That meant
  `kustomize build ./overlays/prod | kubectl delete -f -` was
  incorrectly classified as safe in any case where another token sat
  between `build` and `|`. Tightened to `(?!.*\|)` so any downstream
  pipe — regardless of what comes between — disqualifies the safe-path
  match and lets the destructive `kustomize-pipe-delete` pattern win.

src/packs/kubernetes/kubectl.rs
  In the new pattern-coverage test, the assertion for
  `kubectl delete pods --all-namespaces` was checking for "ALL
  namespaces" in the reason text. But `--all-namespaces` is matched
  *first* by the broader `delete-all` pattern (its regex
  `--all\b` hits the `--all` prefix of `--all-namespaces` because `-`
  is a non-word character). That pattern's reason text says "ALL
  resources", not "ALL namespaces". Adjusted the assertion to match
  the pattern that actually fires; the `-A` short-form test on the
  next line still asserts "ALL namespaces" since `-A` only hits the
  `delete-all-namespaces` pattern.

Both changes are scoped to existing pattern/test code; no new patterns
or test cases.
